sanskrit: बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते। वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः॥
transliteration: bahūnāṃ janmanām ante jñānavān māṃ prapadyate | vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā sudurlabhaḥ ||
literal_meaning: After many births, the one in knowledge surrenders to Me, realizing 'Vāsudeva is all'; such a great soul is very rare.
contextual_meaning: True psychological integration—where every inner fragment is owned and aligned with the Self—requires repeated cycles of trial, error, and reflection before the ego finally releases omnipotent fantasies and bows to a unifying Source within.
psychological_state: Post-integration humility after many identity crises; ego no longer claims authorship.
trigger_scenario: After decades of therapy, meditation, and setbacks, a person finally admits, 'I don’t have to run the show; life is living through me.'
anchor_text: Keep learning until every part of you quietly bows to the One within.
